events.text: DC broker: ~30% of the pipeline is genuinely grid-islanded BTM and counterparty credit (big-four vs Oracle/neocloud) gates the contracting as much as power does. Read-through: supports TLN's 'value of steel in the ground,' but a reminder that the FOTM/co-location regulatory path remains the swing factor.

Q1 FY26: $473M of adjusted EBITDA, $350M of adjusted FCF — EBITDA more than doubled and FCF ~quadrupled YoY on the Freedom & Guernsey acquisitions (~2.8 GW CCGT), higher spark spreads, capacity/RMR revenues since June 2025 and the AWS ramp. The fleet generated ~16 TWh at a 55% capacity factor (Cole Muller).

2026 guidance reaffirmed: adjusted EBITDA $1.75-2.05bn, adjusted FCF $0.98-1.18bn — excludes the pending Cornerstone acquisition (~$500M run-rate EBITDA, >$4/sh incremental FCF), close expected 'as soon as this summer.' Preliminary outlook: ~$34/sh FCF in 2027, ~$36 in 2028 base; ~$41 in 2028 with 70% of FCF to buybacks.

Pre-funded Cornerstone with $4bn of senior unsecured at ~6.25% and retired $1.2bn of 8.625% secured notes — ~$40M/yr of interest savings (~$1/sh FCF), cutting secured debt from ~60% to ~30%. Net leverage 3.1x at 3/31, target <3.5x. $2bn buyback authorization through 2028; $100M repurchased in Q1.

Single nuclear asset concentration (Susquehanna) and merchant exposure — spark spreads and a widening West Hub-PPL zonal basis (possibly non-fundamental) move FCF/sh materially; management calls the basis 'temporal.' PPL zone peak load projected +70% over five years, AEP +30%.
BTM expert (ex-GE Vernova): behind-the-meter is a permanent solution, not a bridge — read-through to the co-location/FOTM debate central to the TLN thesis.
Midstream DC expert: BTM at ~40% of power deals, with ~15-30% peak-shaving post grid-reconnection.

JPM cites Talen's Susquehanna as the cautionary case of BTM co-location regulatory risk — FERC's 2024 rejection of the Talen/AWS BTM agreement, after which Talen pivoted to a front-of-meter arrangement (the ~1.9 GW AWS PPA structure). Deal table: Talen-Amazon Susquehanna PPA expanded 1.92 GW 20 years to 2042 (6/11/25) + original 960 MW (3/4/24). Backdrop: 138 GW of DC growth, $5.5tn of capex, power the binding constraint.

cells: +3% weather-adj YoY; 'value of steel in ground'
Forwards just ticked up, recent phenomenon
$100M in Q1; 'at scale over time,' ~11% FCF yield
Submitted >2 GW to PJM cluster; RVP timing pulled to fall
